mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier credit conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the easing is broad rather than a one-off cut.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income expectations and tighter labor-market conditions, which should depress cyclical equity prices and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE up-moves of at least 0.2 pp are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign relationship reverses for any sustained 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS rise together, retail sales growth is supported by labor income gains, implying stronger near-term consumption demand and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and PAYEMS up does not outperform either series alone for 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal's win rate falls below 52% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO after a prior slump indicates improving industrial output and inventory stabilization, which should support risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for two consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO improvement is not followed by higher-than-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional average return is not positive in at least 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound in HOUST reflects stronger construction demand and household balance-sheet confidence, which should improve cyclical earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above the 3-month average are not followed by positive excess 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over any 4-year span.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL falls while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, real policy becomes tighter and risk assets should weaken, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines on a 3-month annualized basis while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus restrictive FEDFUNDS does not correspond to weaker 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined condition fails to beat a coin-flip baseline over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series after prior gains signals payroll slowdown before it appears in the unemployment rate, and that weakening labor momentum should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS contracts for two straight months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS declines are not followed by weaker 60-day SPX returns than the unconditional average, or if the signal shows no negative skew in at least 70% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration across output and consumption, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth both turn negative versus their prior readings
failure_condition: Reject if joint negative GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not precede below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction signal is not materially stronger than either series alone.
